name: tsl_npc_transfer_provenance
description: [TSL S7] NPC transfer provenance: NPC physically delivers an object to the player. TSL transfer_signal must capture from_actor as the NPC reference. If CB misclassifies as environment_interaction, the source_signals will lack any npc_transfer origin, revealing the provenance gap. Probabilistic.

tsl_expected.notes: NPC (courier) presses a sealed letter into the player's hands — canonical npc_transfer scenario. CB must classify transfer_origin as 'npc_transfer' (NPC is the causal agent; player input did not request the item). TSL transfer_signals should carry from_actor = courier NPC reference (not 'environment'). If CB misclassifies as environment_interaction, source_signals will lack a npc_transfer signal and from_actor will point to environment — this is the provenance gap TSL surfaces. Probabilistic: narrator output determines whether the NPC transfer is written clearly enough for CB to classify correctly. Non-firing (no transfer_signal) may mean the narrator did not write the handoff. Firing with environment_interaction from_actor = CB misclassification.

transfer_signals.notes: from_actor should reference the courier NPC, not environment. If environment_interaction appears in source_signals or from_actor is not an NPC reference, that is the misclassification signal.

world_prompt: I am at a message post at the edge of town. A courier in riding leathers stands behind the counter, holding a sealed letter bearing my name.

turns.label: founding
turns.action: I look around the message post.

turns.label: receive_letter
turns.action: I approach the counter and identify myself to the courier.
